files.snippet:
Summary
Axtrax Pro integrates with KONE Destination Control Server using API 500 Connectivity Suite over HTTPS.
Axtrax Pro retrieves fixed configuration data from the KONE server, then sends user cred...
files.content:
Summary
Axtrax Pro integrates with KONE Destination Control Server using API 500 Connectivity Suite over HTTPS.
Axtrax Pro retrieves fixed configuration data from the KONE server, then sends user credentials and elevator access rights back to KONE.
Elevator control is done via KONE smart readers and the KONE controller. Rosslare panels still handle doors and gates.
System requirements
Valid annual license with an expiry date (required by KONE).
Axtrax Pro v28.0.2.8 or higher.
Network connectivity to the KONE server (IP or URL) with correct credentials.
Core KONE concepts (as seen in Axtrax Pro)
Access Domains: Elevator groupings (by lobby, zone, area) that determine which elevators a user can use.
Access Rights Groups: Floor sets within an access domain that a user is allowed to access.
Time Zones: Schedules defining when floors are accessible.
User Categories: KONE-defined user classifications (similar to departments).
Home Floors: Optional single-floor assignment for users who only need one floor.
Call Types (behavior when card is used): 
Normal
Handicap (longer door open)
Priority
Empty car
Space allocation
Setup: connect Axtrax Pro to KONE
In Axtrax Pro, open the new Elevator Control section.
Click Edit to open the Elevator Configuration window.
Enter server details and credentials, then Connect to retrieve configuration from the KONE server.
Data retrieved (read-only in Axtrax): Access Domains, Access Rights, Time Zones, User Categories, Home Floors, Call Types.
User setup in Axtrax Pro (Elevators tab)
Elevator access rights can be configured for existing users, new users, or visitors.
Credential format limitation noted in training: 56-bit Wiegand cards only.
Fields and settings
Elevator User ID: Issued by the KONE server after saving (initially blank).
Elevator Settings: Which KONE server connection to use (can support multiple buildings).
User Category: Select the KONE-defined category.
Home Floor: Optional.
Call Type: Choose desired elevator behavior.
Access rights assignment flow
Open a user → Elevators tab.
Set the user’s credentials (56-bit Wiegand).
Choose elevator settings, user category, home floor (optional), and call type.
Add Access Domains and corresponding Access Rights Groups.
Set validity date range for elevator access rights.
Click OK to save and push to KONE.
Validation / verification
After saving, confirm that an Elevator User ID appears for the user.
Use the Validate button (available after save completes) to verify settings on the KONE server.
Review the events log at the bottom of Axtrax Pro for success or failure messages.
Architecture notes (what talks to what)
KONE smart readers with touchscreens are required for elevator control. They communicate with the KONE elevator controller.
Rosslare AC-825 panels handle door and gate access and do not connect to elevator control.
Axtrax Pro remains the management point for assigning both door access and elevator floor permissions.
Q&A: known challenges and roadmap
Local KONE teams may not know which API version (500, 1000, 5000) is installed at a site.
To confirm API details, contact the site’s KONE server administrator.
Current integration uses API 500.
API 1000 integration is expected in the coming months.
Future API 5000 is expected to shift more management from the KONE server to the Axtrax Pro server.

files.snippet:
MIFARE Card Security Levels
CSN (Card Serial Number) Mode
All MIFARE readers initially read only the card serial number (CSN or UID)
Available as 4-byte or 7-byte UIDs
No encryption or security - can ...
files.content:
MIFARE Card Security Levels
CSN (Card Serial Number) Mode
All MIFARE readers initially read only the card serial number (CSN or UID)
Available as 4-byte or 7-byte UIDs
No encryption or security - can be read with any NFC-enabled phone
7-byte UIDs always start with "04"
Many readers still operate in CSN-only mode out of the box
Security level comparable to a Kwikset door key - easily cloned
Sector Mode
Uses 48-bit security key (called "Key A" in MIFARE terminology)
Cards can be formatted into sectors (max 16 sectors on 1K cards)
Each sector can have a unique key
Data is not encrypted by default unless specifically encrypted when written
Designed to allow multiple applications on one card (medical info, banking credentials, etc.)
48-bit keys can be cracked in a few hours with proper equipment
Used in some biometric readers to store biometric data on the card
O2S Encrypted Cards
Adds 128-bit encryption on top of 48-bit key
Data is encrypted and format is locked
Significantly more secure - cracking takes much longer
Could not be copied at common card-cloning kiosks
Company maintains control of encryption keys and sells pre-programmed cards
Reader Models and Capabilities
K35 Reader
Reads CSN by default (both 4-byte and 7-byte UIDs)
Has setting to "Ignore first byte on 7 byte CSN" to match other readers
Sector mode available but not enabled by default
Configured through settings interface
Must specify sector, block, and Key A to read secured data
K-30 Reader
Present in the facility
Appears to be an earlier model
6255/6300 Series (B Series)
Originally CSN-only readers (4-byte only initially)
B series models support sector mode via configuration card tool
Configuration tool available for download
Still sold as CSN-only readers for customers wanting simple, inexpensive solutions
Card Structure and Programming
MIFARE Card Organization
Cards organized into sectors and blocks
Example: 16 blocks per sector
Programming requires specifying sector, block address, and Key A
Data written in hexadecimal format
Available in 1K, 4K, 8K, and 16K capacities
Multi-Application Use Case
Theoretically, one card can hold credentials for multiple locations
Each location uses different sector or address within sector
Requires coordination of formats across locations
Rarely implemented in practice
Security and Cloning Concerns
Vulnerability of Basic Cards
CSN-only MIFARE cards as vulnerable as 125 kHz HID/EM cards
Easily cloned with Android phones and apps like Tag Writer
Kiosks in grocery stores can clone 125 kHz cards, driving interest in MIFARE
Many people incorrectly assume MIFARE is inherently more secure
HID Comparison
HID iClass uses similar sector-based approach with proprietary formats and controlled keys
HID now using 256-bit encryption on higher-end cards
Original HID patents expired, making HID prox cards free-for-all
HID more dominant in US market; MIFARE more popular internationally
HID Corporate 1000 formats still proprietary with custom formatting
Configuration Tools and Resources
Mobile Apps for MIFARE
NXP Tag Info app (Android only) - displays card information and format
NXP Tag Writer app - can write data to cards
iPhone NFC radio not accessible for these purposes
Configuration Card Tool
Used to configure 6255/6300 series readers for sector mode
Downloadable from company website or Rossler Security
Home Automation Integration
NFC tags can be used with Home Assistant and similar systems
Same MIFARE credentials work with company's NFC readers
NFC stickers can serve as credentials (1K tags have enough memory for business card data)
All NFC and MIFARE operate at 13.56 MHz frequency
Historical Context
Legacy Issues
Old sector readers (ASB 01 programmer) had firmware issue reading data backwards
Could not be fixed without ordering 1,000+ readers for custom firmware
Very few customers used sector readers until recent years
Recent Adoption
MIFARE reader usage has increased significantly in last few years
Still relatively uncommon in some market segments

files.snippet:
Product Line Evolution
The 6255 series CSN Select readers replaced an older product line that had model numbers like K-H-J-25
The older models looked identical to current AYK-12 units in form factor b...
files.content:
Product Line Evolution
The 6255 series CSN Select readers replaced an older product line that had model numbers like K-H-J-25
The older models looked identical to current AYK-12 units in form factor but were fully potted with no removable backs
When the company started, there were 12-14 different models including the 20 and 25 series, with variations K, H, J, L, and Q (anti-vandal metal housing)
Most variations were plastic molded units that looked identical from the outside - you had to check the back label or wiring to differentiate
Most older models have been discontinued except the AYK-12
Protocol and Feature Differences
The model 14 used clock and data protocol, which was more common historically but is rarely used now
The model 20 was multi-functional for reading EM cards with a control lead that could switch between three output modes: Wiegand (floating), RS-232 (grounded), or clock and data (tied high)
Despite the 20's functionality, the simpler and cheaper model 12 sold better
All the multi-protocol functionality from the 20 was eventually rolled into the 12C, which has been available for years
The company kept the "12" model number because it was well-known and sold well, rather than rebranding as a new 20 series
Form Factors
L form factor is a tall mullion reader (L for "long")
The J form factor was in-between K and L lengths but has been discontinued
The L version is still produced for some models like the 6255
Some old part numbers may still appear in documentation though the products haven't been manufactured in years
L23G Remote System
The L23G is a receiver for remote fobs, similar in concept to garage door openers
Each fob generates a unique 26-bit code like a credential
The receiver has two Wiegand outputs (A and B) allowing control of two different doors based on which button is pressed
Two-button remotes output different codes per button by default (e.g., 1201 and 1202)
Four-button remotes output unique codes per button but all on channel A only
A clever installation used button IDs with access groups in software to control multiple doors, with each button mapped to a different door
Florida Condo Installation Case Study
First deployment of AC-825 panels at a high-end condo in Florida used for valet parking garage access
The remote system was used by valets to open various parking garages across approximately eight panels
The multi-button setup created chaotic event logs with access denied entries on all panels except the authorized one
Installation was sold through EPCOT initially, and Mariano came from South America to help install (Brad sponsored the visa)
The 825 panels had issues due to improper RS-485 wiring spread across Miami
RS-485 Wiring Challenges
Industry specifications called for RS-485 wiring with 120 ohm, 18 gauge twisted pair cable, but this cable does not exist
The closest available option is Belden's RS-485 cable: 22 gauge, twisted pair, controlled impedance, 120 ohm, with drain/ground wire
This specialized cable is expensive, so most installations don't use it
There was a long-running debate about cable specifications, with Mickey insisting on 18 gauge
18 gauge cable is power wire with near-zero impedance, unsuitable for RS-485 data transmission
The company eventually approved Cat5 or Cat6 cable with proper wiring: data on one pair, ground on another twisted pair, with unused conductors grounded to reduce noise
The Florida installation had used 18 gauge twisted wire that couldn't carry RS-485 signals beyond 100 feet (total run was ~1000 feet)
The system was flaky until complete rewiring was required
The installer was initially upset but appreciated having a clear explanation of the problem
Firmware Update and Troubleshooting Practices
Recommending firmware updates is often intentional, not busy work - it clears corrupted flash memory in panels
Legacy panels especially can develop memory corruption from electrical issues, lightning, or glitches over time
Firmware updates clear all panel memory, which often resolves persistent issues
If one component is corrupted, there are likely more problems - firmware update catches all issues at once
Mercury boards have a similar practice: rebooting the panel pushes the entire config block rather than just changes, which can resolve corrupted components like schedules
In the past, firmware updates were risky to perform, but current practices are safer
Office Setup Note
A door alert system was set up for Terry so she would know when someone entered through the front door, particularly when working alone in the mornings

files.snippet:
Firmware and Software Compatibility
Current software enforces firmware version matching, unlike previous versions which allowed running antiquated firmware with newer software
Mismatched versions can ...
files.content:
Firmware and Software Compatibility
Current software enforces firmware version matching, unlike previous versions which allowed running antiquated firmware with newer software
Mismatched versions can cause problems that are difficult to troubleshoot; rolling back software can be problematic as it often affects SQL server/database
Firmware files are located in the server's program files under a firmware folder - this represents the minimum firmware version required for the software to work
Firmware doesn't change between every software release; older panels like the 215 haven't had firmware updates in 5-6 years
Newer firmware with older software generally works fine (unlike the reverse), particularly with 825 panels where firmware changes frequently
Installation and Update Process
The setup program (e.g., G20.20) can be extracted and run manually; extraction is the first step the installer performs anyway
All components are embedded in the install package, including SQL server and firmware files
When manually installing SQL first, the installer will still prompt for SQL installation - select "skip" in this case
Newer firmware can be copied into the firmware directory to update panels to the latest version
Port Forwarding Troubleshooting
Use U-Get Signal (or similar tools) to check if port 1818 is open at remote sites
Disable the network in software temporarily before testing to avoid false readings from active connection attempts
Can test remote IP addresses from any location using the tool
Documentation Resources
UL listed product documentation is available in a cached location on the system
Documentation includes product warranty documents and UL certificates dating back to 2015
B series panels (including 825) are covered in consolidated UL documents, though boards themselves aren't marked with "B" - only part numbers and boxes are
Many readers and components historically were not UL listed

Access Groups & User Management
Key Difference Between Pro and NG
XTracks NG limitation: Each user can only be a member of one access group 
If you have 40 offices with unique access requirements, you...
files.content:
Access Groups & User Management
Key Difference Between Pro and NG
XTracks NG limitation: Each user can only be a member of one access group 
If you have 40 offices with unique access requirements, you need 40 separate access groups
Different schedules require different access groups
Example: A company with 95 employees ended up with 93 access groups due to varying schedules and access needs
XTracks Pro Advantages
Users can be assigned to multiple access groups simultaneously
Build access rights from modular groups (common doors + specific areas)
Can assign individual readers to users with custom schedules
Example workflow: Assign common exterior doors group, then add specific lab access (Lab 1, Lab 2, etc.) as needed
Much cleaner management for large organizations
Time Zones & Schedules
Each access group has a default time zone for all readers
Individual readers within a group can have different time zones via dropdown selection
Migration Considerations
Importing from NG brings over all legacy access groups and structures
Recommended approach: Build new modular access groups and gradually migrate users
Multi-site implementations: Create site-specific groups first, then build role-based groups (e.g., "Fifth Street Manager," "Fifth Street Employee")
Naming conventions become critical for long access group lists
Security Features
Lockdown Functionality
Requires separate lock and unlock cards
Different users can have lock-only vs. unlock privileges
Immune users can be designated to maintain access during lockdowns
Must designate an emergency unlock input on 825 panels (required in many European countries)
Physical unlock button required by European regulations; physical lockdown button prohibited
Lockdown can be triggered from software with proper operator permissions
Common implementations: City/county offices and police stations
Door Interlocks (Mantrap)
Requires door contacts
When one door is open, the other cannot be unlocked
Common for jewelry stores and marijuana dispensaries
825 panels support multiple interlock groups with three or more doors
Card Plus Card
Requires two cards swiped sequentially on same reader for high-security areas
Card plus card groups allow any two people from designated group to unlock door
Access Control Features
Access Areas & Anti-Passback
Used primarily for anti-passback: must swipe out before swiping in again
Supports muster reports to track who's in the building
Car Parking
Controls who can park in specific areas and when
Tracks vehicle counts in parking areas
Can trigger outputs when lot is full (e.g., "Lot Full" sign or gate disable)
Visitor Management
Visitors treated like a department with customizable access
Can specify manager/host and visiting department
Auto-disable options: When visitor exits area or building
Card handling: Set to inactive, unauthorize user, or unassign card to pool
User Administration
User Filter Tool
Quick search by name, department, user number, or card number
Search by card ID (facility code not needed) to identify card owners
View all users in specific access group
Identification field (125 characters) is searchable - useful for apartment numbers or grouping
Card Automation
Automatically delete or inactivate unused cards after specified period
Options: Notification only, queue for approval, or automatic action
Generate reports of unused cards for cleanup
ID Card Printing
Built-in card design tool with drag-and-drop interface
Supports portrait or landscape orientation
Can include: User number, name, department, title, photo, logo
Prints to default Windows printer
Set card printer as default before opening client
Can save designs and batch print
Operators & Permissions
Current Pro Version
All operators must be mapped to a user
Web client access requires license (one included, additional licenses available)
Can now reset passwords with admin rights (unlike NG which requires deleting/recreating operator)
Password requirements: Uppercase, lowercase, number, minimum 6 characters
Permission Levels
Event view: Modify time frames (read-only vs. none)
Network access controls
Lockdown permissions allow operator to apply/release lockdowns
Status Maps & Monitoring
Visual Door Monitoring
Add doors to map in design mode
Show lock relay status or door monitor (open/closed)
Customize fonts and icon sizes
Can import background drawings (limited file types, not PNG)
Scaling challenges - best to build on display machine for large monitors
Live status with right-click door control (unlock, lock, open permanently)
During lockdown: Can unlock specific door temporarily to allow emergency access
Reporting
Immediate Reports
Who's been in today
Last known position
Roll call/muster
Panel Reports
View any event type (inputs, outputs, access events)
Sort by user or reader
Time range options: "Last day" = yesterday (not last 24 hours); use Custom for 24-hour periods
Event types: Access granted, access denied, access logged, access recorded
Custom fields can be added
Does not

1. Introduction
Rosslare’s EX-07/EX-17 electronic REX buttons control the access of a Figure 1: EX-07 Figure 2: EX-17

duty, non-sparking, non-magnetic, corrosion resistant, illuminated, anti-

They feature solid state Piezoelectric Switch Technology generating 2

Among the many features of the EX-07 and EX-17 are: Jumper selectable

The EX-07 and EX-17 mount directly to standard US-size gang

files.content:
MD-N33
Installation Instructions Data Communication Modem
1. Introduction
The MD-N33 is a data communication modem intended for data
transfer over worldwide PSTN networks. It is intended to link RS232
based serial communication devices over dial-up telephone lines
in applications where these are located on remote areas.
The MD-N33 is a V.34/33.6K modem complies with worldwide
telecom requirements. The modem is AT Command set compatible
and can be configured from any PC based communication
program
The following pages outline the installation and configuration
instructions for your new MD-N33 modem.
2. Features
• V.34/33.6K • Buzzer for call progress monitoring
• AT command compatibility • LED indication of telephone line status
• 60-character command line buffering • LED indication of power supply
• Auto-dial, redial, and auto-answer • Intelligent DAA technology detects line status
• Pulse or tone dial • Industry-standard error correction and data compression
• Remote hang-up detection • DTMF detection and distinctive ring
• Digital PBX detection and protection • Non Volatile RAM (memory) to store user profiles
• Global approvals • Line protection circuitry included
3. Technical Specifications
3.1 Data Characteristics 3.2 Electrical Characteristics
Data format: Serial RS232 interface : -
Input Power 10 16 V DC
Data - V.34, V.32bis, V.32, V.22bis, V.22, V23, V.21, Bell
Power Consumption: Typical: 70 mA
212A & Bell103
Maximum: 100 mA
Error Correction: V.42 (LAP-M or MNP 2-4)
Buzzer: For call progress monitoring
Data Compression: V.42bis, MNP 5
LED - Blue LED indication of telephone line status
Fax: V.17, V29, V.29ter, V.21 ch.2
Class 1 and 1.0 commands Red LED indication of power supply
Modes of Operations: Full duplex over dial-up lines; data mode,
command mode, 3.3 Environmental Characteristics
Online command mode Operating Environment: indoor use
Advanced -Extension pickup detection, remote hang-up Operating Temperature: 0°C to 63°C (32°F to 145°F)
detection, line-in-
Operating Humidity: 0 to 95% (Non-Condensing)
Use detection, digital PBX detection and protection
Flow Control - XON/XOFF (software), RTS/CTS (hardware)
3.4 Physical Characteristics and Interface
Command Buffer - 60 characters
Dimensions: 110mm(4.3")x63mm(2.5")Wx39mm(1.5")
Telephony - V.253 commands: V.253
2-bit and 4-bit ADPCM, 8-bit linear PCM, and 4-bit Weight: 0.214 Lbs. (97g)
IMA coding 8 kHz sample rate Phone - RJ-11 jack for phone line
Concurrent DTMF, ring, and Caller ID detection Data - D9 Male connector, RS232 link:
1 – DCD 2 – RD 3 – TD 4 – DTR
5 – GND signal 6 – DSR 7 – RTS
8 – CTS 9 – NC
4. Connection and Configuration
4.1 Hardware Requirements Power Jack
The following are the required hardware units used to connect the
MD-N33 to either the PC or remote Unit.
• Crossed linked RS-232 cable - 9 pin D type female plugs on
both sides.
RJ-11 Jack
• Standard telephone cable- RJ11 plugs on both sides.
• 12V (200mA) DC power adaptor, connected to the unit's DC
Figure 1: Front View of the MD-N33
jack.
• MD-N14 RS485 to RS232 Converter (for RS485 units)
4.2 General:
Both Md-N33's, the one connected to the remote unit and the one
connected to the PC need to be configured while connected to D9-RS232 Jack
the PC, prior to remote installation.
Figure 2: Rear View of the MD-N33
1 0706-0960110+00
4.3 PC Side Connection 8) Connect the MD-14 directly to the MD-N33 DB9 jack.
1) Connect a 12V (200mA) DC power adaptors to the MD-N33. Ensure
that the Power LED (red) is on.
2) Connect the MD-N33's D9 RS-232 jack to an available COM port on 4.5 Network Connection Diagrams
the PC using a crossed Serial Cable.
3) Connect the MD-N33 to the telephone line using a standard telephone
RJ-11 cable.
4) Configure the modem using the networked software
(AS-215 or DCC)
4.4 Remote Connection
After configuring the MD-N33 for the remote unit connect it as
PSTN
follows:
5) Connect a 12V (200mA) DC power adaptors to the MD-N33. Ensure
that the Power LED (red) is on.
6) Connect the MD-N33 to the telephone line using a standard telephone
RJ-11 cable.
7) Connect the unit to Rosslare's MD-14 (RS485 to RS-232 Converter)
using the 4 wire cable.

1. Introduction
The CSN SELECT™ AY-x6255 is a series of multi-credential technology 1.1 Installation Kit
contactless smart card readers for use in access control system
The installation kit consists of the following items to be used during
solutions. CSN SELECT readers have the capability to read the Card
the installation procedure:
Serial Number (CSN) from many smart card RFID Standards
implementations. The following list shows the credential technologies  1 self-adhesive mounting label template
for which we have confirmed compatibility:  2 mounting screws and 2 screw anchors
 ISO14443A – MIFARE® Ultralight® Nano / EV1/ C, MIFARE  1 Torx key tool
Classic® / Classic EV1, MIFARE Plus® S / SE / X / EV1, MIFARE
 1 Torx security screw
DESFire® EV1 / EV2, N-TAG NFC / Card Emulation
Figure 1: AY-x6255
 ISO14443B – China National ID
 ISO15693 – HID® iClass®, PicoPass, iCode, LEGIC
 ISO18092 – SONY® FeliCa® (Hong Kong Octopus)
 Rosslare’s CS-ECA NFC app (HCE) for Android smartphones
Scan the QR code to download Rosslare’s NFC app.
This list is continuously updated. For the latest compatibility list,
contact your sales representative

AC–115
Compact Networked

SINGLE DOOR CONTROLLER. IT PROVIDES EXTREMELY COST-EFFECTIVE SOLUTIONS...
files.content:
AC–115
Compact Networked
Single Door Controller
THE AC-115 IS A COMPACT, POWERFUL, QUICK AND EASY TO INSTALL ALL-IN-ONE NETWORKED
SINGLE DOOR CONTROLLER. IT PROVIDES EXTREMELY COST-EFFECTIVE SOLUTIONS FOR SMALL
NETWORKED APPLICATIONS OF UP TO 8 DOORS. THIS UNIT CAN CONTROL 2400 USERS (IN/OUT),
WITH ADVANCED SECURITY RULES AND FEATURES, AND INCLUDES USER-FRIENDLY PC SOFTWARE
FOR MANAGEMENT, CONFIGURATION, MONITORING, AND REPORTING. THE AC-115 INCLUDES A
BUILT-IN SOUNDER FOR DOORBELL, CHIME AND ALARM SOUNDS, AND A BUILT-IN BACKUP
BATTERY CHARGER.
GENERAL DESCRIPTION  Real-time clock with backup battery lasts up to
Each AC-115 supports a single door with power and 10 years
control for 2 readers (IN/OUT), a 1.2 A lock strike, 
Cable-cut tamper detection function for readers
door monitor & REX input, and has a built-in backup
and cover tamper detection for the main unit
battery charger.

Bell, chime & alarm annunciator with audible
With the PC software (AS-115), the system works
tones
seamlessly in online and offline modes. A 2000 event
offline FIFO history log is stored in non-volatile

3 selectable security modes and 3 programmable
memory that can be uploaded to the PC. user levels
Up to eight controllers can be connected in a reliable 
AC-115 can work as an independent single-door
and proven RS-485 network that interfaces with the
PIN controller and does not require the PC
PC software using one RS-232 to RS-485 converter.  For Microsoft Windows® 98/ME, and XP/NT/2000
It is compatible with a variety of Rosslare’s readers,
and third-party magnetic lock strikes to suit the

A simple and intuitive user-friendly PC interface
application with a Form C locks strike relay for fail- reduces complexity of managing access control
safe or fail-secure installations.  Long range RS-485 network connects to the PC
Unique features include internal bell sounder using a MD-14 RS-232 to RS-485 converter (one
enabling doorbell, chime, and alarm sounds. per network)

Enables management of user data, photo with
MAIN FEATURES
custom fields, access rights, alarms, strike time,

2400 users and door mode from a central location for up to
 Quick and easy to install, All-in-One 8 networked doors
 Supports two standard Wiegand 26-Bit readers  Produces reports from acquired data, such as
(PIN only, PIN+PROX, or PROX) entry and exit times, and by alarm type with filters

Internal power supply for readers, lock and
for users, location, and time
inputs, with a charger for external battery backup

Available in a large selection of languages
SPECIFICATIONS
ELECTRICAL SPECIFICATIONS

Input Power 16 VAC (1.5 A, 25 VA) from a transformer

Input Current
Standby: 65 mA (not including attached devices)
Maximum: 120 mA (not including attached devices)

Relays
Strike: 5 A Form C, N.O.
Auxiliary: 1 A N.O. closed to ground

Battery Charger 12 V Sealed Lead Acid (SLA) type up to 7 Ah

Indoor Single-Door Standalone Proximity & PIN Controller
AC-B32 is part of Rosslare’s series of indoor standalone controllers, ideal for
residential and commercial access applications. It is an industry favorite for
those looking for a basic access controller that simply gets the job done, yet
utilizes both proximity cards and/or PIN codes.
The AC-B32 is easy to program and easy to use. This Proximity & PIN standalone
controller is packed with features such as multiple user levels and operation
modes, programmable siren and lock strike release times, code search, and
back and case tamper protection.

Rosslare Security Products, a division of Rosslare Enterprises Ltd., has been manufacturing high-quality security products

The ultimate access
control platform
Secure. Flexible. Scalable
The Rosslare market-proven flagship networked control panel AC-825IP is well
positioned at the heart of Rosslare access control solution...
files.content:
The ultimate access
control platform
Secure. Flexible. Scalable
The Rosslare market-proven flagship networked control panel AC-825IP is well
positioned at the heart of Rosslare access control solutions. Optimized to meet
the full range of today's customer requirements, the controllers feature 6 to 58
reader configurations with a variety of expansions and a proven installed base
of more than one million doors across the globe.
Boasting sophisticated design and performance to fit projects of any size
and complexity, Rosslare controllers are ideal for new installations, as well as
replacement of existing systems. The controllers are comprehensive, modular
and versatile, and can be easily scaled to handle anything from a single facility
to multiple remote premises.
All controllers are equipped with professional enclosure, and are fully powered
by our QxControl Video management and access control platform as well
as AxTraxPro™ access control management platform that enables setting
AC-825IP
automated physical access control policies across facilities, perfect for
today’s most demanding users and organizations. QxControl and AxTraxPro™
Networked Control Panel can manage thousands of Rosslare networked control panels capable of
supporting hundreds of thousands of users, providing enhanced security with
excellent system scalability and performance.
Flexibility powered by security
The AC-825IP 6-door networked control panel communicates securely using AES High number of access points per single AC-825IP controller, supporting 12
256-bit encryption, with the management platform over TCP/IP connected to expansions via OSDP Secure Channel protocol
all access points, enabling implementation of the required access policy within
Offline Mode Operation – access control policy and door automation
a protected area. The AC-825IP features a wide range of expansions, making it
schedulers keep working even when network interruptions occur
ideal and cost-effective for projects of any size, from small to large or multi-site
Up to 16 credentials per user with a wide range of built-in access policy
with a high door count. The AC-825IP offers all the features of the QxControl
options included
and AxTraxPro™ platforms, with digital logic, non-volatile memory, and a backup
battery to continue implementing all access rules even even without a continuous Antipassback rules to prevent illegal access by door, by time or global
connection to the network or power.
Easy configuration of Access Point interlock rules allow the creation of simple
two-door "man traps" and highly customizable interlock scenarios in groups
Advanced communications including SNMP V3 and “Push” allow reduction of
network load, improved efficiency and higher security
Secure, lockable power-management enclosure with built-in illumination bar
Speed of installation – designed without compromise to make installers’
lives simpler while reducing installation time and complexity, allowing quick
troubleshooting and system maintenance
Power plug lock and power level status logging and monitoring using
AxTraxPro for AC Mains and UPS Battery operation

Key features and advantages
R-805 S-805 D-805 P-805
Adds Adds Adds Adds

16 outputs 16 inputs 4-door 16 inputs &

Designed with installers’ needs in mind, the AC-825IP has everything that professional
installers need in a scalable networked controller. State of the art bi-directional
push communication and expandability with plug and play expansions bring system
configuration flexibility to a new level. Equipped with a highly functional, stylish
enclosure with built-in illumination bar, smart cable management, split power supply
and many other features – the AC-825IP stands out from the crowd.
The AC-825IP is compatible with 4 types of expansion boards that communicate
over OSDP. Supports either 6 readers via OSDP or 4 Wiegand and 2 OSDP readers
simultaneously.

files.content:
The Future-proof
Transition Reader
Sleek. Versatile. Robust.
Rosslare introduces the Multi-Smart™ mini mullion AY-K35, a multi-
technology reader with water resistance, vandal-proof and anti-microbial
properties, offering versatility and scalability to secure your physical access
control today and into the future.
Including 125 kHz, 13.56 MHz & MIFARE Classic Sector credentials, with NFC
and BLE mobile credentials, to power your systems and user experiences.
Equipped with OSDP secure channel, AY-K35 makes migration to advanced

organization. The reader is wired to the controller and can be controlled by

files.content:
AY- V12
Medium-Range Proximity Reader
The compact AY-V12 offers the best solution for medium-range RFID proximity
readers for standard passive proximity cards and tags at an affordable cost.
Aside of its small size and ease of use, this reader provides the same features
that come standard in all of Rosslare's readers and controllers – high quality,
durability, and aesthetic design.
GENERAL DESCRIPTION PROFESSIONAL GRADE FEATURES
The AY-V12 is the low-cost solution for security 
RF Modulation: ASK at 125 kHz
situations that require a compact, medium-range


proximity reader.

It is both easy to install and easy to use, and the
state-of-the-art design and small footprint is ideal

Internal buzzer
for indoor/outdoor access control.  LED control input
The AY-V12 features an output format of standard 
Optical wall tamper detection
Wiegand 26-Bit, which allows it to work with most
controllers. For added convenience, the reader also

Suitable for outdoor use (IP54)
contains two LED indicators that show the unit’s  Made of tough polycarbonate UV-resistant
status upon power up and the status for card plastic
reading attempts.
MAIN FEATURES

Wiegand 26-Bit output format

RFID read range up to 22 cm (9 in.)

Operating voltage range of 5 to 16 VDC

46-cm (18”) pigtail cable
SPECIFICATIONS
ELECTRICAL SPECIFICATIONS


 LED Control Input Dry Contact, N.O.
OPERATIONAL SPECIFICATIONS
 Output Format Wiegand 26-Bit
 Audio/Visual Bi-colored LED indicator, built-in sounder

Design
Epoxy-potted, fully-sealed in a rugged polycarbonate enclosure.
Suitable for harsh environments.
ENVIRONMENTAL SPECIFICATIONS
 Operating Environment Water resistant, suitable for outdoor use (IP54)
 Operating Temperature Range -32ºC to 63ºC (-25ºF to 145ºF)
 Operating Humidity Range 0% to 95% (non-condensing)
 RFI Protection > 20 V/m up to 1000 MHz
PHYSICAL SPECIFICATIONS
 Dimensions (H x W x D) 125 x 125 x 21mm (4.9 x 4.9 x 0.8 in.)
 Weight 285 g (10.1 oz)
The AY-V12 is compatible with a variety of Rosslare's controllers, as
SYSTEM COMPONENTS
well as with many third-party access control systems.
PRODUCT WARRANTY 5-Year Limited Product Warranty
ABOUT ROSSLARE SECURITY
Rosslare Security Products manufactures and markets high-quality security products via its worldwide offices and channel
partners. Since 1980, Rosslare has offered high-quality systems for enterprise, small business, and residential
applications. With Rosslare, you receive the best of all worlds: world-class product engineering and design; professional
customer service spanning the globe; and the quality and affordability of a vertically integrated and self-owned
manufacturing facility. Our expansive product range features much more than access control solutions and guard patrol
management systems; we also offer applications software – such as License Plate Recognition, Time & Attendance, and
DVR/alarm integration.
www.rosslaresecurity.com

Rosslare Security Products, a division of Rosslare Enterprises Ltd., manufactures high-quality security products since 1980. The
company’s three main lines – Access Control, Intrusion Detection, and Guard Patrol – together with a growing product range
have transformed Rosslare Security into a major worldwide force in security. Rosslare holds itself to the highest standards of
customer service and manufacturing (ISO 9001:2008, ISO 14001:2004, ISO 13485:2003). The company complies with the EU
Directive 2002/95/EC on Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS).

AY-x2B
125 kHz EM Contactless
Smart Card Reader
AY-M2B AY-H2B AY-L2B AY-K2B
The AY-x2B series is a 125 kHz reader capable of reading ID cards, tags, and credentials
based on the EM contactless card technology. It reads the unique ID (UID) of the credential
and transfers it via Wiegand 26-Bit data to the access control panel.
The AY-x2B series comes with a wall mounting screw kit and top cover, and the board is
coated with conformal coating to protect it from the elements and provide a long service
life. It is a very simple, reliable, and cost-effective way to upgrade your system to the smart
card standard.

AYC-B7661
MIFARE Classic® EV1 Full-Finger Capacitive
Biometric Reader/Controller
Rosslare’s AYC-B7661 is a standalone biometric reader/controller.
The AYC-B7661’s sensor verifies between fingerprint data and a biometric
template stored in the unit. The AYC-B7661 is convertible; it can work as a

Backlit goPROX® & PIN Reader – Convertible Series
The go PROX® AYC family is a series of convertible PIN/Proximity readers. The go
PROX® AYC series automatically determines whether to function as a reader or as
a secured standalone controller. If the unit is connected to a standard access
control unit, then it functions as a reader. If the unit is connected to a Rosslare

Protection down to the microbe
For nearly three decades, Rosslare has grown steadily in the global access control
market, leading the path in innovative products an...
files.content:
MicroFree™
Antimicrobial
Technology
Protection down to the microbe
For nearly three decades, Rosslare has grown steadily in the global access control
market, leading the path in innovative products and solutions. Our engineers and
designers invest much time and effort thinking about our customers and end
users needs, discovering new technologies and trends to make access safer
for everyone.
Doors and access control have become the focal point of humans and vehicles,
with traffic being redirected to managed access points. As a result, access
points have become contaminated; and high density areas that challenge facility
operators increase cost of ownership. Keeping these areas clean has become a
high priority for small to large organizations.
Rosslare has partnered with a leading provider of registered antimicrobial
technology that has a long history of safe and effective use. Introducing Rosslare
MicroFree™ technology.
Built-in antimicrobial safeguard
Rosslare MicroFree™ unique technology features antimicrobial and antifungal
properties that offer advanced protection, maintaining 24/7 carefree operations
while keeping all access points safe and clean – with up to 99.8% reduction in
microbes. The process starts in Rosslare’s factory during production, while the
antimicrobial additive is mixed with the plastic material before injection. This
provides the user with complete protection from microbes.
By reducing the transfer of degrading microbes across surfaces, the risk of cross
contamination lowers dramatically, keeping employees and customers safer in a
cleaner environment.
Rosslare antimicrobial solutions are ideal for facilities that require a higher level of
hygiene, such as hospitals, laboratories, surgeries, food manufacturing and more.

Protected by Rosslare
Unprotected MicroFree™ Antimicrobial
surface Technology*
* Available on selected readers

files.content:
AxTraxPro
A ccess Control Manag ement
Software
V28.1.1.x
Datasheet
1. Introduction
Rosslare's AxTraxPro™ client-server software management platform enables setting automated physical access
control policies across facilities, perfect for today’s most demanding users and organizations. The system can
manage thousands of Rosslare networked access controllers and users, providing enhanced security with
excellent system scalability and performance.
AxTraxPro offers a friendly web-based and mobile application1 interfaces that allow system operators to locally
or remotely manage their on-premise AxTraxPro servers.
Integrated with video management systems and combining Rosslare state-of-the-art access control components,
including controllers, readers, credentials, and other peripherals, the complete ecosystem is optimal for almost
any need.
2. Main Features
AxTraxPro enables organizations to meet their dynamic needs with agility and performance like never before.
Leveraging a set of features designed to maximize utility and security, AxTraxPro includes new tools to save time
and resources when building the physical access control environment.
• Mobile app1 and web clients: For on-going management of the on-premise AxTraxPro server, operators can
access the system from their web interface and mobile phones to perform daily tasks.
• Operator role-based access control: Ensures operators only have access to the information and resources
necessary to perform their specific tasks functions in AxTraxPro.
• Multi-factor authentication: AxTraxPro allows MFA using identity, password and one-time tokens to ensure
a Zero Trust security model.
• Multi-site support: AxTraxPro provides advanced operator permission controls that enable efficient creation
and management of multi-site environments and serve multi-tenet environments.
• Secure remote Access: Web clients enable secure remote access through a zero-trust approach. This allows
authorized users to manage on-premise systems remotely without exposing servers to the public internet or
relying on traditional VPNs.
1 Supported by AxTraxPro up to version 28.0.5.2.
AxTraxPro V28.1.1.x Datasheet 1
• Enhanced security: Communication is secured from the credential all the way to server using advanced
encryption algorithms.
• Lockdown2: Manage and activate lockdown door groups for emergency situations.
• Video integration: Integrated with major NVR vendors, including Hikvision and Dahua, for event-based video
pop-up and snapshot reports.
• Integrated biometrics: Integrated with Rosslare devices, IDEMIA SIGMA & STid Architect (Bio on card),
IDEMIA VisionPass & Vision Pass SP series, and Hikvision MinMoe face biometric devices to enable
improved facility security.
• Centralized user management:
Enhanced access control policies such as access groups, interlock groups, Antipassback and more.
Managing user information, photo, access credentials and related access policies, using new secure
login process with dual-factor authentication dialog.
LDAP and LDAPS integration to enable secure and easy user management.
• Up to 16 credentials per user: Supports Face-ID, Fingerprint, PIN-Codes, RFID cards, UHF Tags, NFC-ID, BLE-
ID, QR Code and LPR for vehicles.
• Integrated Elevator system: Integrated KONE elevator destination control system to enhance security and
people flow management.
• Integrated Intercom system: AxTraxPro integrates with the basIP LINK cloud-based intercom solution to
improve communication and entry management.
• Alarm management and reporting: Alarm management workflow across devices and smart links
automations, email notifications and reports module.
• Ironclad software security2: Client-server security mechanism including AES 256-bit encryption
communication to ensure secure data in transit. Sensitive data is fully encrypted in the database to provide
data security in rest that meets privacy regulation requirements.
• Time and attendance: Built-in capability of generating attendance reports with preset formats and
automated customized reports.
• AxTraxPro plugIn for Milestone XProtect: Enables Milestone XProtect® to act as the frontend system,
providing operators with the ability to manage access control events and alarms through the XProtect VMS.
• Car parking management: Integrated with 3rd party and Rosslare products for automated gate opening and
touchless experience. Including UHF readers, license plate cameras, and mobile credentials, along with
fundamental features related to parking occupancy management and control.
• REST API: Provides the ability to integrate and deploy with third-party applications. This interface allows for
full system access functionality, and to control and monitor all aspects of a site access requirements.
2 Only with the AC-825IP controller.
AxTraxPro V28.1.1.x Datasheet 2

files.content:
Biomuseo,
from conception
to inception
A museum’s quest for security
and technology
In a museum, access control is not the only issue; sensitive and confidential areas are
separated from public areas, valuable assets are safeguarded, and employee time clocks
need to be managed. The Biomuseo in Panama is securing its perimeters with Rosslare’s
Readers and Controllers.
Biomuseo’s perimeters are a complex environment, with multiple high-traffic buildings
to control, each divided between public and secure areas, and complex division between
employee, subcontractor, and specialist access, with time and attendance management
required. Furthermore, Biomuseo possesses high-value assets and rare animals that
require security and protection. Their desire was to restrict access to authorized personnel
to administrative offices while allowing marine biologists access to the sea research
institute, with its hatcheries and aquarium areas, without compromising on security to
protect expensive and sensitive information, equipment, and infrastructures.
CASE STUDY
Carlos Arsenio Muñoz Flores, Technological Director of the museum since its foundation in 2014, chose to answer
the museum’s pain points with Rosslare equipment. Since technology is at the heart of the museum, Flores selected
Rosslare’s readers, controllers, and cloud-based AnTraxNG management software for its competitive advantage,
modular access control system, and flexible and effective installation.
In order to control the museum’s doors and facilities, the Biomuseo team has
chosen Rosslare AC-225IP, AC-425IP, and AC-825IP networked controllers, About Biomuseo

Smarter access
for a safer campus
Invisible security for hands-free
and child-safe access
Colegio Nueva Granada (CNG), located in Bogotá, Colombia, is one of the country’s
most prestigious international schools, with over 800 students from more than 70
nationalities. Recognized for its academic excellence, cultural diversity, and strong
commitment to student safety, CNG is endorsed by the U.S. government for its
security standards and is situated near the Israeli embassy. Many children of former
presidents, ambassadors, congressmen, and business leaders attend the school,
making security a priority of the highest order.
CASE STUDY

files.content:
Securing
Academic Excellence
How Rosslare's access control solution
is empowering Agustiniana University?
The university complex consists of two distinctive campuses: the Tagaste campus,
which includes 3 buildings with three-story each, and the Hipona campus, which
includes 1 building with four-story each. Over 7,000 people populate the campuses
daily, creating heavy peak-hour traffic and security-related challenges. Agustiniana
University partnered with RCM, Rosslare's official Colombian partner, to manage
access control and secure their premises seamlessly. Together, they built a security
policy allowing over 7,000 staff and students to enter the campus daily.
CASE STUDY
yticrevinU
anainitsuguA
©
The university required a system to monitor and control access to classrooms, laboratories, and restricted areas.
Additionally, they wanted to limit access to authorized staff only at sites containing valuable equipment and sensitive
information. Finally, they needed a vandal-proof system to handle complex daily operations.
With their extensive background in access control, RCM and Rosslare proposed AxTraxNGTM, a real-time management
solution for institutions on several campuses. Using AY-K25 and DR-6255 readers with 40 advanced networked controllers,
Rosslare installed AxTraxNGTM access management software, controlling close to 200 doors in an organized way that
monitors the entry and access of staff and students in detail. The system allows the division of cardholders according
to their standing, with administrative staff limited to office buildings and teachers and students to their classrooms,
laboratories, and recreational facilities.
In conclusion, Rosslare provided Agustiniana University with a durable and vandal-proof system that efficiently manages
detailed access control and reports on daily uses for staff and students, integrating security into the university premises.
About Augustiniana Univercity
Agustiniana University in Bogotá, Colombia, has been a non-profit institution since
1996, committed to guiding students and staff in their physical, intellectual, and
spiritual development. The university values academic excellence and promotes
knowledge generation, transfer, and appropriation in all fields based on ethical
values and morals. Additionally, it contributes to sustainable development, societal
transformation, overall quality of life, democracy, and peace.

The CP-R27 is a new contactless smart card and desktop programmer to read/write 13.56 MHz technology,
including MIFARE Classic EV1 and MIFARE DESFire EV1/2/3. It is compliant with ISO14443A/B.
Using the CP-R27 from Rosslare, installers, and customers can easily program on-site their credentials and
prepare the configuration card for configuring their readers.
The CP-R27 is designed as a Plug & Play smart card programmer with a USB port, and is suitable for various
smart card applications, such as smart card issuing and programming, personal identification.

The ultimate access control challenge
in cash transport
Cash transport services face significant security challenges in protecting assets during

files.content:
Reinventing
Armoured Fleet
Protection
The ultimate access control challenge
in cash transport
Cash transport services face significant security challenges in protecting assets during
transit. A leading armoured transportation company in the UAE sought an advanced access
control solution for its fleet of hundreds of vans. The company required a system capable
of securing onboard safes, preventing unauthorized access, maintaining seamless server
communication, and ensuring continued operation even during network outages. With
valuable cargo and operational efficiency on the line, finding the right solution was critical.
The challenge was multifaceted. Compliance with strict MOI (Ministry of Interior)
regulations meant that the solution had to include a reliable interlock system, allowing
only one door to open at a time while ensuring multi-level authentication for personnel
verification. The outer door required a rugged, anti-vandal reader to withstand extreme
environmental conditions, while the inner door needed a biometric authentication system
with encrypted credentials for maximum security. Additionally, the system had to integrate
seamlessly with the company’s existing security infrastructure while offering offline
functionality to maintain operations in case of network failures.
CASE STUDY
Rosslare, in collaboration with 4Force Security Solutions, engineered a comprehensive, high-security access control
system tailored for the armoured fleet. The outer door was secured using the AYC-Q6355 anti-vandal Smart Card reader
with a backlit keypad, providing dual-factor authentication and high durability against physical tampering and harsh
weather conditions. This ensured that only authorized personnel could initiate access procedures. The inner door was
reinforced with the AY-B9250BT biometric fingerprint terminal, featuring a liveness check and encrypted credentials for
fast, highly secure authentication. This advanced biometric solution prevented unauthorized entry, even in cases of lost or
stolen credentials.
Both access points were controlled through
Rosslare’s AC-825 access control panel, a
powerful and flexible security hub. The entire
system was seamlessly integrated with the
AxTraxPro™ management platform, enabling
real-time monitoring, remote access control, and
automated security policies. Even in the event
of a network disruption, the system’s offline
functionality ensured that operations remained
uninterrupted, maintaining a strict security
AYC-Q6355 AY-B9250BT protocol across the fleet.
Anti-Vandal Smart Card Reader Fingerprint
Biometrics Reader
The implementation of Rosslare’s solution
significantly elevated the security framework
of the company’s cash transport operations. The interlock system, aligned with MOI regulations, ensured that no
unauthorized access could occur at any stage of the process. The outer reader’s anti-vandal design provided long-
term reliability, while the biometric inner reader added an extra layer of security, eliminating risks associated with lost or
duplicated credentials. With AxTraxPro™, security teams could remotely monitor vehicle access, track compliance, and
optimize fleet operations with confidence.
By leveraging Rosslare’s cutting-edge technologies, the company successfully deployed a scalable, highly secure, and
regulation-compliant access control system. This case stands as a testament to the importance of advanced security
solutions in the evolving landscape of cash-in-transit operations, where every second and every layer of protection counts.
About 4Force Security
4Force Security is a trusted provider of advanced Access Control systems, delivering
seamless security for businesses, government entities, and large-scale enterprises.
Designed and engineered by security professionals, 4Force’s scalable solutions range
from standalone access points to complex multi-site infrastructures.
4Force Security is committed to delivering future-ready solutions, integrating cutting-
edge technology with tailored security systems to meet clients’ unique needs. 4Force’s
dedication goes beyond installation as 4Force builds long-term partnerships, providing
exceptional support, continuous innovation, and reliability.

about Rosslare solutions for your Access Control Project?

files.content:
Networked Control Panels
The ultimate networked
control panel solutio n
with flexibility built-in
The Rosslare market-proven series of networked control panels are at the
heart of Rosslare access control solutions. Optimized to meet the full range of
today's customer requirements, the controllers feature 2,4 and 6-reader models
with a variety of expansions and a proven installed base of more than a million
doors across the globe.
Boasting sophisticated design and performance to fit projects of any size and
complexity, Rosslare controllers are ideal for new installations, as well as the
replacement of existing systems. The controllers are comprehensive, modular
and versatile, and can be easily scaled to handle anything from a single facility
to multiple remote sites.
All controllers are equipped with a professional enclosure, and are fully
powered by our AxTraxPro™ access control management software that enables
setting automated physical access control policies across facilities, perfect
for today’s most demanding users and organizations. The AC-825IP controller
is also compatible with our QxControl cloud-based video management and
access control platform, providing an advanced, unified solution for seamless
monitoring and control. Both AxTraxPro and QxControl can manage thousands
of Rosslare networked control panels supporting hundreds of thousands of
users, delivering enhanced security with excellent scalability and performance.

files.content:
Secure your access points
the touchless way
during the COVID-19 epidemic
Secure Access Solutions
Secure your access point s
the touchless way
Coronavirus has made contactless access solutions a must-have for hospitals, quarantine zones, work
environments and more. But not all contactless solutions are equally compliant and effective.
Today more than before, contactless access solutions are essential to secure clean areas and prevent the spread of
the virus. Rosslare brings over four decades of industry expertise, tailoring reliable contactless access solutions to
wide ranging organizations, giving Coronavirus-oriented projects our top priority to keep society safe & healthy.
Complete range of access solution s
to make everything touchless
We are Access Control specialists, offering everything you need to secure your access points during the COVID-19
epidemic and beyond. From the moment you leave home and throughout your day at work, our hardware and
software can keep you safe and protected – in one complete solution.
Management Touchless readers & Advanced network
software control face recognition terminals controllers

files.content:
Rosslare Cards & Credentials
From technology
to form factor and beyond
A wide range of open and
secure credentials
Rosslare offers a large selection of high-quality credentials
compatible with today’s most popular devices, ideally suited for
various applications and environments. The credentials offering was
created to cover organizations’ needs – from technology to form
factor – while featuring long-lasting product quality for peace of mind
and cost savings.
Rosslare’s rich product portfolio includes both low and high frequency
credentials: legacy 125 kHz EM and Temic, MIFARE Classic, Plus
and DESFire EV1 that operate in 13.56 MHz and provide high-level
security, and UHF Alien Higgs-3 credentials that provide a great
reading distance. All credentials are available unprogrammed or
preprogrammed with optional inkjet printing or laser engraving.
In addition to physical credentials, Rosslare offers mobile credentials
solutions that leverage users’ smartphones as credentials, allowing
access to doors without using physical keys. The solutions utilize
BLE (Bluetooth Low Energy) and NFC technologies, combined with
a Rosslare mobile app, as well as an SDK that allows developers to
integrate the technology and turn any 3rd party iOS and Android apps
into a mobile key with only a few lines of code.
13.56 MHz 13.56 MHz 13.56 MHz 125 kHz
MIFARE® DESFire® EV1 MIFARE Plus® MIFARE Classic® EV1 EM & Temic
UHF Alien Mobile Mobile
Higgs-3 Credentials App Credentials SDK

files.content:
DR-6255
CSN SELECT™ Desktop Enrollment Reader (Rev. B)
The DR-6255 is a desktop smart card CSN enrollment reader with a USB
interface for use when enrolling user credentials to access control software
such as Rosslare’s AxTraxNG™. The reader is part of the CSN SELECT™ series of
multi-credential technology contactless smart card (13.56 MHz) readers that
support access by an NFC compatible Android smartphone app*. This reader
has the flexibility to read the Card Serial Number (CSN) from many smart card
RFID standards implementations, perfect for transitioning from one
technology to another. CSN SELECT readers are able to read the CSN from
many smart card RFID standard implementations. The reader has a sleek and
modern design, and is powered by the USB port itself for easy and convenient
installation.

Integrate your business
Development
seamlessly with
Tools
our flexible solutions
Satisfy your clients’ needs by integrating with other infrastructure
and technologies while enhancing data integrity and reducing
costs and time. This paper gives an overview of Rosslare’s well-
documented and simple-to-use development tools that are available

requirements, the EX-06 will adapt well in mines, oil rigs and refineries,

Rosslare Security Products manufactures and markets high-quality security products via its worldwide offices and channel

Advanced

leading global

Whom do you call to secure 20,000

hundreds of visitors?
When faced with the dilemma, FPT Softwa...
files.content:
Advanced
access control
solution for
leading global
IT provider
Whom do you call to secure 20,000
sqm, six floors, 5000 staff members, and
hundreds of visitors?
When faced with the dilemma, FPT Software trusted only the leading company Rosslare to
secure their premises with top-of-the-line readers and controllers.
When FPT Software recently integrated its new premises in Danang City, they searched for
a provider that would understand the complexity of its 6-floor, 20,000 sqm campus. They
naturally turned to our partner Khai An Co., Ltd, known for providing Rosslare’s leading
technology in the area.
CASE STUDY
In their high-traffic environment, with over 5000 staff members on-premises, in addition to their customers, visitors, and
partners, FPT Software required a top-of-the-line access control system that could manage and secure their complex
vulnerabilities. The system needs to limit access to the campus, buildings, rooms, facilities, and physical assets to
authorized personnel while monitoring and reporting the staff’s and individuals’ access to the restricted business locations
and secured areas. Furthermore, it must be integrated with the fire alarm system that operates a pre-defined group of
doors that will automatically be opened in an emergency. Finally, due to the complex and dynamic nature of the security
task, the required access control system needs to be highly versatile while easy to use, allowing maintenance of pre-
defined levels of security on a single platform.
About
FPT Software
Established in 1999, FPT
Software is a globally
leading technology and IT
services & solutions provider
headquartered in Vietnam,
+ +
with 54,000 employees in 28
AC-215IP
countries. With international
expertise spanning over
a quarter of a century,
FPT is a pioneer in digital
transformation, delivering
world-class services to over
1000 clients globally.
Enter Rosslare’s AxTraxNG as the central access control platform, guaranteeing a system that is easily manageable while
answering the complexity of the security and access control policy. Comprised of AC-215IP networked Controllers and AY-
M26 Mifare Classic Sector Readers, the front end of their premises is highly secured by the proprietary set of readers and
cards, which have been programmed with their sector keys. To increase security and prevent breaches, dual authentication
is required for their high-value assets, such as the data centers, implemented with the deployment of AYC-W6500 readers.
With over 200 panels to secure, control and manage, and the forecast of another 200 odd access points, FPT Software is
satisfied with the state-of-the-art access control system implemented and confident they will continue with Rosslare’s
constantly expanding line of hardware, and software offers for their future expansion.
Want to know more
about Rosslare solutions for your Access Control Project?
Contact us: sales@rosslaresecurity.com
www.rosslaresecurity.com

files.content:
High-capacity
T&A & Throughput
Management
for over
5000
Employees
In its quest to create automated processes, GRSE partnered with Rosslare to solve a
process that integrates face readers and AC-825IP multi-door access controllers.
Founded in 1884 as a small privately owned company on the eastern bank of the Hooghly
River, Garden Reach Shipbuilders & Engineers Ltd, abbreviated as GRSE, is one of India's
leading shipyards, located in Kolkata, West Bengal. It builds and repairs commercial and
naval vessels. Today, GRSE builds export ships on a mission to expand its business and
has facilities in Kolkata and Ranchi.
With over 5,000 users between their 1,500 employees and 3,500 contract workers, GRSE
needed a 360-degree approach that would seamlessly integrate, on the one hand, the
access control to its facilities and the Contract Labor Management System (CLMS), and on
the other hand the existing ERP, HRMS & Payroll software. When Rosslare was approached,
GRSE was manually processing the Contract Labor Registrations, Verifications, Approvals,
and Terminations and requested a solution that would automate the procedures.
CASE STUDY
Employees and contractors must be managed according to shifts, labor
access control, and work permit processes, which are very time-consuming
Rosslare Face Readers
and laborious. The solution Rosslare proposed combined a Biometric Face
installed are elegant and
Reader with an AC-825IP multi-door controller and management software to
efficient at the same time, and
address all pain points.
our working has become very
smooth after the complete
Rosslare's Android-based Face Reader is one of the most advanced
system implementation.
biometric readers available in the market, with a wide range of wide range of
Thanks to Rosslare and SIPL
authentication options in one device, including Face, Finger, Card, and PIN
Team for all the support.
code. With fast walkthrough technology, users can walk in and get verified.
Furthermore, as contract workers are not permanent, face readers were the Mr. Rajender Banerjee, GRSE
preferred solution, with their capability of authorizing 100 users in 5 minutes
in a challenging environment.
+ +
AC-825IP
Moreover, Rosslare's AC-825IP is an advanced multi-door controller that can manage dozens of readers at the same
time and store 100,000 users and 500,000 transaction logs each. It works as a master controller connected to multiple
expansions and controls multiple doors simultaneously. AC-825IP is packed with advanced features such as lockdown,
smart interlocking, encrypted communication, and more.
Finally, integrating the Rosslare SDK software solution with existing ERP, HRMS & Payroll software resulted in a
seamless registration process that manages the contractor and contractor's labor.
Garden Reach Shipbuilders & Engineers Ltd
GRSE is known as India's leading shipyard, commercial, and naval vessel builder.
In 2006 it was awarded the Miniratna status and was the first Indian shipyard to
have built over 100 warships.
Want to know more about Rosslare solutions for your Access Control Project?
Contact us: sales@rosslaresecurity.com
www.rosslaresecurity.com

Access Control
To enter with safety
When it comes to gate automation and providing access to your facility via an access
point, we provide access control solutions that you can trust. Whether securing your
customer’s warehouse, residential, commercial property or your parking areas, our
solutions are affordable, scalable, easy to use and install, while keeping your
premise safe and secure.

Video Camera
From a single access
UHF Long-Range
Reader

complex systems, our

an effective and efficient

control policy for gates.
Long-Range
RF Receiver
Hands Free and Automatic Gate Control & Operation
For hands free and automatic gate control and operation, our innovative
long-range UHF readers are: AY-U920BT that reads various UHF tags from
a distance of up to 12 m (39 ft) vs. AY-U915BT that reads from a distance of
up to 6 m (19 ft). These readers are also empowered by BLE technology and
mobile apps enabling easy access to vehicles and pedestrians without UHF
AY-U920BT AY-U915BT
tags or when a dual factor authentication is required.
Remote Gate Operation
For remote gate operation we offer AY-L23, a long-range RF receiver combined
with SA-23-B2 and SA-23-B4 remote controls that operates from distance of
up to 70m (230 ft). This device also operates as a stand-alone controller that
opens the gate without additional software.
SA-23-B2 SA-23-B4
AY-L23
License Plate Recognition (LPR)
For vehicles, we offer a LPR solution that is an integrated part of our software
suite allowing you easy and intuitive management of your access point, while
any vehicle will be automatically recognized and authorized to access the
facility.
LPR
Access Control Software – AxTraxNG
With our advanced AxtraNG software platform you will be able to define a
complete set of rules for gate’s operation including operation time, control,
schedulers and much more. This will allow you to fully manage any access
point, supervise vehicles and pedestrians’ entries to enhance site’s security.
Access Car Parking Residential Fleet
Control Rental Management Buildings Management
Securing your customer’s warehouse, commercial property, and parking areas
does not have to be difficult or complicated.

Rosslare’s LK-M03/M06L/M12L high quality magnetic locks are ideal for secure
applications where access is restricted, such as office buildings, hospitals, and
airports. They have a holding force of 300/600/1200 pounds, respectively, and
are easy to install. The attractively designed Maglocks are surface mounted,
operate on dual voltage, 12 VDC or 24 VDC, and have low current
consumption. These Maglocks are compatible with EN and UL standards.

Seamless Integration of Security, Visitor
Management, and Elevator Control
Located near the successful Sarona Gardens in central Tel Aviv, Landmark TLV project adds
mixed-use s...
files.content:
Redefining
Urban Access
Seamless Integration of Security, Visitor
Management, and Elevator Control
Located near the successful Sarona Gardens in central Tel Aviv, Landmark TLV project adds
mixed-use spaces for offices, commercial, and residential purposes. New office towers are
positioned along the site’s edge, enhancing the Tel Aviv skyline and creating a public plaza
with retail spaces that integrate with the area. Beneath the complex, a subterranean road
connects to six underground parking levels. The new buildings form one of the city's largest
office complexes, with the larger tower consisting of two connected buildings featuring
a vertical atrium and internal bridges. These open, sunlit spaces foster visual and spatial
connections for occupants, improving the quality of interior office environments. The
buildings’ exterior features a double-skin curtain wall with diamond-shaped extrusions,
which protect the interiors from the sun while offering panoramic city views.
CASE STUDY
48
oidutS
:gniredneR
stcetihcrA
rahsaY
:stcetihcrA
VIVA-LET
KRAMDNAL
:gniredner
eht
ni
eman
tcejorP
Enhancing Tower Security and Residence Access
I would like to thank
The challenge was to implement a seamless access control system for restricted floors,
Rosslare, a Synerion
combining KONE Office Flow elevator destination control with standard elevator access.
company, and the entire
The system needed to integrate with CoreVision's Visitor Self Registration Kiosk and allow
team for their excellent
access for residents, employees, and visitors using a single interface and credential.
cooperation and the
great investment in
implementing the API
integration between
our companies.
The joint work and
professionalism
you demonstrated
throughout the project
enabled us to execute
the solution smoothly
and efficiently. We
appreciate your
commitment to the
project's success
and look forward to
continued fruitful
cooperation in the
future.
Haviv Ronen,
KONE
KONE, as the main integrator, utilized AC-825IP control panels and AY-K6255 readers
over OSDP for the standard elevators. Working with Rosslare’s development team, the
integration of AxTraxPro with KONE Office Flow was completed in a short timeline. This
enabled management of elevator destination control and speed gates access through
AxTraxPro’s interface, providing unified access rights and restrictions for elevators and
floors using a single credential. The integration, certified by KONE, provided Landmark
with a seamless solution for access control, visitor management, and elevator
destination control.
In addition Rosslare's professional services group facilitated the integration with CoreVision,
enabling QR credentials for elevator access to be assigned to visitors.
Rosslare was selected for its comprehensive planning, product quality, and customization services. The integration of
AxTraxPro with KONE’s elevator control system and CoreVision’s visitor management was delivered smoothly, ensuring
efficient management of access control and elevator systems.
Want to know more
about Rosslare solutions for your Access Control Project?
Contact us: sales@rosslaresecurity.com
www.rosslaresecurity.com

MD-25TB

THE MD-25TB IS A SECURE RELAY I/O MODULE THAT CONVERTS ROSSLARE’S CONVERTIBLE SERIES (AYC) READERS
INTO SECURE STANDALONE CONTROLLERS. WITH THE SECURE MODULE INSTALLED INSIDE THE SECURE PREMISES,
CRITICAL WIRING FOR INPUTS, OUTPUTS, REX, AND LOCK ARE INSIDE THE SECURE PREMISES AWAY FROM EXTERNAL
MANIPULATION. THE SECURE MODULE SUPPLIES POWER TO THE CONTROLLER AND UTILIZES A PROPRIETARY
TWO-WAY PROTOCOL TO COMMUNICATE WITH THE CONTROLLER. THE MD-25TB MAY BE WALL MOUNTED OR
INSTALLED IN THE ME-0515M MULTIPURPOSE POWER MANAGEMENT ENCLOSURE.

MD-D02B
2-Reader Expansion Module
for AC-225x-B Control Panel
Rosslare’s MD-D02B is a plug-in expansion module for Rosslare’s AC-225x-B access control
panels that provides a 2-reader interface, 4 supervised inputs with end-of-line (EOL) resistor
protection, and 4 relay outputs (5 Amp Form-C N.O./N.C.) to the panel. Installation of the
module takes under one minute with the tailored expansion port in the panels and auto-
recognize by the panel. Once it is installed and the panel is powered on, the expansion is
recognized by Rosslare’s AxTraxNG™ or AxTraxPro™ server, and the MD-D02B can be
programmed for access control and automation. Advanced communication with the controller
allows for firmware update and real-time event logging, with support for full offline operation
by the host panel.

files.content:
MD-N32
RS-232 to TCP/IP Gateway
Rosslare's MD-N32 is a powerful, high performance gateway designed to send
and receive information between varied pc-managed access control system
products via a LAN or WAN, thus saving on serial cabling installation. Integrally
compatible with the AxTraxNG software, the MD-N32 gateway packs advanced
features such as ping, search on LAN, variable baud rate, data type, MAC/IP
address, and communication parameters. One unit can handle multi-door sub-
networks (RS-485/232) connected via TCP/IP to the AxTraxNG server.
The MD-N32 gateway delivers simple, reliable, and cost-effective network
connectivity for multiple serial devices.
GENERAL DESCRIPTION MAIN FEATURES
Rosslare's MD-N32 Gateway is designed with a 
Universal RS-232 to TCP/IP gateway for general
focus on high quality and reliability as our top
use
priority.

Supports DHCP dynamic IP addressing
As a compact plug-in module, the MD-N32 easily
connects to most products without modification.

+12 VDC jack power input connector

converts it to TCP/IP format, and sends it over the 

LAN or WAN to the PC. From the other end,
communications are sent from the PC over the

device.

AC-215, AC-225, or AC-425 panels

instructions). Once the module is configured, the
or directly using AxTraxNG software, AYC-

W6500 as well as intrusion alarm panels

applications requiring serial port environments and
network device management.

Rosslare Security Products manufactures and markets high-quality security products via its worldwide offices and channel

Wireless Access Control Door Interface
THE MD-W11 IS A QUICK AND INEXPENSIVE SOLUTION FOR CONNECTING REMOTE DOOR DEVICES
SUCH AS READERS, LOCKS, AND REX BUTTONS TO AN ACCESS CONTROL UNIT. THE UNIT UTILIZES

Rosslare offers a large selection of high-quality, long-lasting credentials that are compatible with today’s most

files.content:
MIFARE DESFire EV3
Credentials
Datasheet
1. Introduction
Rosslare offers a large selection of high-quality, long-lasting credentials that are compatible with today’s most
popular devices and are recognized for their high number of possible reads. The MIFARE® DESFire® EV3 smart
card credentials have the highest level of security and are ideally suited for a variety of applications and
environments where security is important while keeping the solution cost-effective.
2. General Description
The MIFARE DESFire EV3 smart card credentials use AES-128 (Advanced Encryption Standard) for authentication,
encryption, and data integrity.
The smart card credentials are available as an ISO card or in a key FOB form factor. For the ISO card, a photo ID
image can be directly printed on the card by inkjet or it can be added via thermal transfer. For a key FOB,
programmable laser engraving of an ID and the facility code is available.
3. Open to Secure
Rosslare’s credentials and programming platform comprises a highly secured line of card readers compliant with
existing access control systems. The platform allows a choice between open key and preconfigured readers and
credentials. The open key and credential management solution for MIFARE DESFire® EV3 includes the tools to
configure everything in-house in order to secure a facility and access by managing reader and credentials settings.
The preconfigured readers and credentials have distinct custom keys and security configuration that are made
during production. This helps to further reduce the cost of ownership. Rosslare provides MIFARE DESFire EV3
credentials either programmed or blank.
Using the Rosslare CP-R27 desktop programmer and KeyCard Studio software, customers can make
configuration cards for the reader settings and quickly make MIFARE DESFire® EV3 credentials with user data.
4. Main Features
• 13.56 MHz Smart card technology
• 2 kB/4 kB/8 kB memory
• Flexible memory structure
• AES-128 for authentication, encryption, and data integrity
• Fast data transfer speed up to 848 kbit/s
MIFARE DESFire EV3 Credentials Datasheet 1
• ISO card form factor with optional inkjet printing
• Shark, twist, and ring key fob form factors with optional laser engraving

AxTraxPro™ Integration for
Milestone XProtect® VMS
Control access and video surveillance,
all from one completely integrated solution
Rosslare AxTraxPro™ is the ultimate end-to-end access control ecosystem
thanks to its exceptional performance, scalability, and ability to integrate easily
with third-party systems.
XProtect®
The integration with AxTraxPro™ enables the Milestone XProtect® to act as the
front-end system and provides operators the convenience to manage access
control events and alarms on the XProtect Smart Client, XProtect Web Client and
Milestone Mobile. Events can be monitored, alarms can be operated, doors can be
opened, and video can be viewed, all from one easy-to-use interface.
Key features Compatible hardware and software products
and benefits
The integration with Milestone XProtect® is available for all devices compatible with the AxTraxPro™
management software.
